message:
I am thinking...: 
The DRT is actively running:
- Design: 227,340 components, 98,581 nets, 6,618 pins, 322 unique inst patterns
- Die area: 4278670 × 3778670 nm = ~4278.67 × 3778.67 µm (a sizeable design)
- Currently in the groups pre-routing phase (completing groups)
- Total groups not yet known - this is normal DRT initialization

The DRT is working through the "groups" phase which handles partitioning the routing problem. After groups, it'll start the actual routing iterations (0th, 1st, 2nd, ... optimization iterations).

This is a big design (227K instances, 98.5K nets). DRT for this scale typically takes 1-4 hours. The process is running at ~384% CPU (using 8 threads as confirmed by `[INFO ORD-0030] Using 8 thread(s).`).

I should monitor it periodically without hogging the terminal. Let me set up a background monitoring approach to check progress every 15 minutes.

args.thought:
The DRT is actively running:
- Design: 227,340 components, 98,581 nets, 6,618 pins, 322 unique inst patterns
- Die area: 4278670 × 3778670 nm = ~4278.67 × 3778.67 µm (a sizeable design)
- Currently in the groups pre-routing phase (completing groups)
- Total groups not yet known - this is normal DRT initialization

The DRT is working through the "groups" phase which handles partitioning the routing problem. After groups, it'll start the actual routing iterations (0th, 1st, 2nd, ... optimization iterations).

This is a big design (227K instances, 98.5K nets). DRT for this scale typically takes 1-4 hours. The process is running at ~384% CPU (using 8 threads as confirmed by `[INFO ORD-0030] Using 8 thread(s).`).

I should monitor it periodically without hogging the terminal. Let me set up a background monitoring approach to check progress every 15 minutes.
